steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage establishes that HILDA studies “trends in economic well-being that can take a long time to develop.” This long development timeline is the decisive detail explaining why HILDA requires more funding than the straightforward fitness study.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A explains that finding long-term trends in economic well-being requires collecting data over many years—the direct reason HILDA demands significant funding.

The other three either shift to fitness (B, C) or raise a sample-size concern the passage doesn't address (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
